  "sinks": "Potential sinks are HTML strings generated by various functions (e.g., <a>, <img>, <h1>-<h6>, <pre>, <code>) which output HTML based on input text and link definitions. These are inserted into DOM or page content.",
  "flows": "Input Markdown text flows through preprocessing steps (line normalization, placeholder replacements), then through block-level processing (_RunBlockGamut), inline span processing (_RunSpanGamut), and finally unescaping. Link/image URLs and titles are derived from user input and link definitions, then embedded into HTML tags. No external network communication occurs.",

The code is a standard Markdown parser implementation with functions for parsing headers, lists, code blocks, blockquotes, inline formatting, and link/image embedding. No obfuscated code or unusual patterns are detected.",
  "analysis": "The script is a comprehensive implementation of a Markdown to HTML converter, including functions to parse headers, lists, code blocks, blockquotes, and inline elements like links and images. It uses regular expressions for pattern matching, with safe handling of user input via escaping functions. The code manipulates input text and generates HTML output without any network activity or system modifications. No malicious behavior, data leaks, or sabotage mechanisms are present. The use of global hashes is standard for storing link definitions and HTML blocks. The code is well-structured for its purpose and appears safe.",
